topic: In China employee termination: role-relay governance map for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination role relay governance

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Core legal source for role ownership boundaries around route and consequence decisions.

sources.title: Regulations for the Implementation of the Labor Contract Law of the PRC (State Council Decree No. 535)
sources.fit_note: Supports practical handoff design between legal, HR, payroll, and management roles.

sources.title: Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Labor Dispute Cases (I)
sources.fit_note: Adds adjudication-facing perspective for evidence and narrative consistency at handoff points.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Anchors payroll/statutory closure ownership in final relay stage.

sources.title: Employee Termination in China: A Guide for Employers
sources.fit_note: Provides multinational-friendly implementation perspective on employer control architecture.

source_replacement_notes: Rotated from cost-risk matrix format to role-relay governance format while keeping legal-source backbone stable.
Excluded low-fit tax-centric and broad comparative sources to keep role-ownership analysis focused.
Retained China Briefing as practical guidance layer for foreign clients.
consensus: Termination failures often occur at cross-function handoffs rather than in isolated single-team tasks.
Legal route clarity, evidence discipline, and payroll/communication alignment require explicit ownership mapping.
Role-relay framing is useful for foreign teams managing distributed decision authority.
uncertainties: City-level implementation practice and evidence emphasis can vary.
Complex payment treatment choices may require local confirmation.
Official English texts may not include all Chinese-language practice nuances.
source_fit_assessment: Sufficient for role-relay format because sources support legal authority, handoff controls, and multinational practical interpretation.
